page: UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a high-capacity virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with an x86_64 architecture running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.4 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a local 500 GB SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a score of 173282.24, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Single-core performance is also strong, with a stress-ng score of 3021.58. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 9301.72 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 262.5 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are outstanding, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 99065.52 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 813.68 GB/sec. Database operations are highly performant, with Redis SET operations achieving 124.66 million operations per second. However, LLM text generation benchmarks show weaker results, with the 7B model generating only 7.85 tokens per second.

Given its high core count and substantial memory capacity, this instance offers a resource-dense profile suitable for demanding enterprise workloads. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, but it provides high qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized tasks that can leverage the massive 80-core architecture. This server is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, large-scale web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and parallel batch processing.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-80xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 80 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 6.4 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes a bundled 500 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 173282.24 and a Passmark CPU score of 109497.78. It also delivers exceptional database performance, processing 124.66 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core and memory bandwidth metrics are strong, LLM text generation speeds remain a relative bottleneck. This resource-dense server is highly suited for parallelized enterprise workloads, high-throughput databases, and intensive web serving.
